prompt.text: You ___ wear a life jacket in the canoe.

answers.text: have to

items.hintDe: "du musst" – die Form für you. Vergiss das kleine to nicht.
items.explainDe: Bei you, we, they und I heißt es have to. Danach kommt die Grundform: have to wear.
items.note: SB ex 3: "you have to ... a life jacket in the boat all the time." (boat untaught → canoe). Distractors: has to (wrong person), have (missing to), must to (no to after must).

prompt.text: She ___ get up early. The camp day is at 7.

answers.text: has to

items.hintDe: "sie muss" – bei he, she, it ändert sich have to. Wie heißt die 3. Person?
items.explainDe: Bei he, she, it heißt es has to (nicht have to). Richtig: She has to get up early.
items.note: Third-person has to. Distractors: have to (wrong person), has (missing to), haves to (no -s on have).

prompt.text: You ___ wash up. The guides do it at this camp.

answers.text: don't have to
do not have to

items.hintDe: "du musst nicht" – etwas ist nicht notwendig. Bei you brauchst du don't.
items.explainDe: don't have to heißt: es ist nicht notwendig (du darfst es aber). Verneint wird have to mit don't / doesn't, nie mit haven't.
items.note: SB box: "You don't have to wash up." Distractors: doesn't have to (wrong person), haven't to / hasn't to (no do-support).

prompt.text: He ___ wear an anorak today. The sun is out.

answers.text: doesn't have to
does not have to

items.hintDe: "er muss nicht" – nicht notwendig, und bei he. Welches kleine Wort brauchst du statt don't?
items.explainDe: Bei he, she, it heißt die Verneinung doesn't have to. Richtig: He doesn't have to wear an anorak.
items.note: Third-person negative doesn't have to. Distractors: don't have to (wrong person), hasn't to / haven't to (no do-support).

prompt.text: ___ you have to wear a hard hat to build a tree house?

answers.text: Do

items.hintDe: Eine Frage mit you. Womit beginnt die Frage – Do oder Does?
items.explainDe: Fragen mit have to beginnen mit Do / Does. Bei you heißt es Do you have to ...?
items.note: SB notice: "Bring a hard hat!" for building a tree house → recast as a question. Distractors: Does (wrong person), Have/Are (wrong question word for have to).

prompt.text: ___ Dana have to do all the camp jobs every day?

answers.text: Does

items.hintDe: Frage über eine Person (Dana = she). Beginnt die Frage mit Do oder Does?
items.explainDe: Bei he, she, it (auch bei einem Namen wie Dana) beginnt die Frage mit Does ... have to ...? Das have bleibt dann ohne -s.
items.note: Story character Dana at the camp. Third-person question Does ... have to. Distractors: Do (wrong person), Has/Is (wrong question word).

prompt.text: It is a camp rule: every child wears a life jacket in the canoe.

answers.text: She has to wear a life jacket.

distractors: She have to wear a life jacket.
She has wear a life jacket.
She haves to wear a life jacket.
items.hintDe: Bei she heißt es has to – und das to darf nicht fehlen.
items.explainDe: Richtig: She has to wear ... Bei he, she, it nimmt man has to (nicht have to, nicht haves to) und das to bleibt.

distractorPool: She have to wear a life jacket.
She has wear a life jacket.
She haves to wear a life jacket.
She have wear a life jacket.
She must to wear a life jacket.

items.note: Context fixes that it is necessary; the contrast is purely the form of has to. All options in-bank.

prompt.text: At this camp the guides wash up, so it isn't your job.

answers.text: You don't have to wash up.

items.hintDe: "nicht notwendig" = don't have to. Achte darauf, dass to nach have steht.
items.explainDe: Richtig: You don't have to wash up. Verneint wird mit don't have to – nie mit haven't to, und das to muss bleiben.

distractorPool: You doesn't have to wash up.
You not have to wash up.

items.note: SB box meaning (not necessary). Targets the do-support negation; all options in-bank function words.

prompt.text: It's a camp rule: every child in a canoe wears a life jacket.

answers.text: You have to wear a life jacket.

distractors: You don't have to wear a life jacket.
You have wear a life jacket.
You has to wear a life jacket.
items.hintDe: Das Schild sagt, es ist notwendig. Welcher Satz drückt "du musst" richtig aus?
items.explainDe: Es ist notwendig → You have to wear a life jacket. don't have to würde "nicht notwendig" heißen, und have / has to sind die falsche Form.

distractorPool: You don't have to wear a life jacket.
You have wear a life jacket.
You has to wear a life jacket.
You doesn't have to wear a life jacket.
You must to wear a life jacket.

items.note: Context (it is necessary) makes only have to right; one distractor flips the meaning, two break the form. All in-bank.

prompt.text: Sam writes to her mum: at this camp the guides do the washing up.

answers.text: We don't have to wash up.

distractors: We doesn't have to wash up.
items.hintDe: Gute Nachricht = es ist nicht notwendig. Welcher Satz sagt das richtig mit don't have to?
items.explainDe: Nicht notwendig → We don't have to wash up. have to wäre "müssen", haven't to / doesn't (bei we) sind falsch.

distractorPool: We doesn't have to wash up.
We not have to wash up.

items.note: SB email (Sam): "we don't have to wash up - that's good." Context fixes not-necessary; distractors flip meaning or break do-support.

prompt.text: She have to wear a life jacket in the canoe.

answers.text: She has to wear a life jacket in the canoe.
has to

items.hintDe: Bei she stimmt die Form von have to nicht. Wie muss es heißen?
items.explainDe: Bei he, she, it heißt es has to. Richtig: She has to wear a life jacket.
items.note: Common error from brief: She have to → She has to.

prompt.text: You haven't to wash up at the camp.

answers.text: You don't have to wash up at the camp.
You do not have to wash up at the camp.
don't have to

items.hintDe: have to wird nicht mit haven't verneint. Welches kleine Wort brauchst du?
items.explainDe: Man verneint have to mit don't / doesn't, nie mit haven't. Richtig: You don't have to wash up.
items.note: Common error from brief: haven't to → don't have to (do-support).

prompt.text: He hasn't to get up early on the free camp day.

answers.text: He doesn't have to get up early on the free camp day.
He does not have to get up early on the free camp day.
doesn't have to

items.hintDe: Bei he wird have to verneint mit ... ? Nicht mit hasn't.
items.explainDe: Bei he, she, it heißt die Verneinung doesn't have to. Richtig: He doesn't have to get up early.
items.note: Common error: hasn't to → doesn't have to (third-person do-support).

prompt.text: You have wear a hard hat to build a tree house.

answers.text: You have to wear a hard hat to build a tree house.
have to

items.hintDe: Ein kleines Wort fehlt zwischen have und wear. Welches?
items.explainDe: Nach have kommt to: have to wear. Richtig: You have to wear a hard hat.
items.note: Common error from brief: omitting to (have wear → have to wear). SB notice: hard hat for tree house.

prompt.text: Do you have wash up after dinner?

answers.text: Do you have to wash up after dinner?
have to

items.hintDe: Auch in der Frage fehlt ein kleines Wort nach have. Welches?
items.explainDe: In der Frage bleibt have to zusammen: Do you have to wash up? Das to darf nicht fehlen.
items.note: Omitting to inside a question (have wash → have to wash).

prompt.text: Your friend doesn't want to do the camp jobs. Tell her: 'You ___ (not / wash up) — the guides do it.'

answers.text: don't have to wash up
do not have to wash up

items.hintDe: "nicht notwendig" bei you: don't have to + Grundform.
items.explainDe: Nicht notwendig → don't have to wash up. Die Grundform wash up bleibt nach to.
items.note: Produce don't have to from a not-necessary trigger. Camp world (washing up).

prompt.text: A new girl asks about the camp rules. Tell her: 'You ___ (wear) a life jacket in the canoe.'

answers.text: have to wear

items.hintDe: Eine Regel ist notwendig: have to + Grundform.
items.explainDe: Eine Regel sagst du mit have to: You have to wear a life jacket. Nach to kommt die Grundform wear.
items.note: Produce have to from a necessity trigger; camp rule about life jackets.

prompt.text: Tell the new boy about Dana: 'She ___ (get up) early because the canoe group leaves at 7.'

answers.text: has to get up

items.hintDe: Bei she und notwendig: has to + Grundform.
items.explainDe: Bei she heißt es has to: She has to get up early. Nach to bleibt die Grundform get up.
items.note: Third-person has to in production; story character Dana, early canoe trip.

prompt.text: She has to wear a life jacket. Ask about the rule.

answers.text: Does she have to wear a life jacket?

items.hintDe: Frage über she: Beginne mit Does, dann she have to ...?
items.explainDe: Richtig: Does she have to wear a life jacket? Bei she beginnt die Frage mit Does, und have bleibt ohne -s.
items.note: Statement → third-person question (Does she have to ...?).

prompt.text: You want the camp rules for tonight. Ask your guide: you / get up early tomorrow.

answers.text: Do we have to get up early tomorrow?
Do I have to get up early tomorrow?

items.hintDe: Frage an den guide: Beginne mit Do, dann we/I have to ...?
items.explainDe: Richtig: Do we have to get up early tomorrow? Bei we / I beginnt die Frage mit Do.
items.note: Form a first-person have-to question from chips; camp world.

prompt.text: Du musst eine Schwimmweste im Kanu tragen.

answers.text: You have to wear a life jacket in the canoe.
You have to wear a life jacket in the canoe

items.hintDe: "du musst" = you have to. "Schwimmweste" = life jacket, "Kanu" = canoe.
items.explainDe: Du musst ... = You have to ... Nach have to kommt die Grundform: have to wear.
items.note: deToEn; SB dialogue about the life jacket in the boat (boat → canoe).

prompt.text: Du musst nicht abwaschen. Die Anleiter machen das.

answers.text: You don't have to wash up. The guides do it.
You don't have to wash up. The guides do it
You do not have to wash up. The guides do it.
You don't have to wash up.

items.hintDe: "du musst nicht" = you don't have to. "abwaschen" = wash up, "Anleiter" = guides.
items.explainDe: Du musst nicht ... = You don't have to ... Das heißt: nicht notwendig (du darfst aber). Nach to kommt wash up.
items.note: deToEn; SB email line "we don't have to wash up". First sentence alone accepted as partial.

prompt.text: to / have / you / a life jacket / wear

answers.text: You have to wear a life jacket.
You have to wear a life jacket

items.hintDe: Subjekt + have to + Grundform + Rest: You have to wear ...
items.explainDe: You have to wear a life jacket. Bei you heißt es have to – die Chips has und must brauchst du nicht.
items.note: 5 answer chips + 2 distractor chips = 7 ≤ 10. Distractors has/must tempt wrong person and wrong modal.

prompt.text: to / has / she / early / get up

answers.text: She has to get up early.
She has to get up early

items.hintDe: Bei she: has to + Grundform. She has to get up early.
items.explainDe: She has to get up early. Bei she heißt es has to – have und must passen hier nicht.
items.note: 5 answer chips + 2 distractor chips = 7 ≤ 10. Distractor have baits the third-person error.

prompt.text: have / does / to / she / a hard hat / wear

answers.text: Does she have to wear a hard hat?
Does she have to wear a hard hat

items.hintDe: Frage über she: Does + she + have to + Grundform?
items.explainDe: Does she have to wear a hard hat? Bei she beginnt die Frage mit Does, und have bleibt ohne -s. do und has brauchst du nicht.
items.note: 6 answer chips + 2 distractor chips = 8 ≤ 10. Builds a third-person question; distractors do/has bait wrong auxiliary.

prompt.text: camp jobs: which rule fits?

pairs.left: You are going out in a canoe.
pairs.right: You have to wear a life jacket.

pairs.left: The guides do the washing up.
pairs.right: You don't have to wash up.

pairs.left: You are building a tree house.
pairs.right: You have to wear a hard hat.

pairs.left: The camp food is free for you.
pairs.right: You don't have to bring food.

items.hintDe: Ist es notwendig (have to) oder nicht notwendig (don't have to)? Lies jede Situation.
items.explainDe: have to = es ist notwendig, don't have to = es ist nicht notwendig. Jede Situation passt zu genau einer Regel.
items.note: Situation ↔ have-to / don't-have-to rule, all camp world, both sides in-bank.

prompt.text: camp rules: have to or don't have to?

groups.label: You have to (it is a rule)
members: wear a life jacket in the canoe
wear a hard hat to build a tree house
be at the gate at 8 a.m.

groups.label: You don't have to (not a rule)

items.hintDe: Sortiere: Was ist notwendig (have to) und was ist nicht notwendig (don't have to)?
items.explainDe: Bei have to ist etwas notwendig, bei don't have to nicht. So teilst du die Camp-Regeln in zwei Gruppen.
items.note: Sorts necessary vs not-necessary actions; mirrors the SB box's two halves. All members in-bank.

prompt.text: Welche Form von have brauchst du bei he, she, it? (er/sie/es muss)

answers.text: has

items.hintDe: Die 3.-Person-Form von have. Drei Buchstaben.
items.explainDe: Bei he, she, it heißt es has to. Die Form von have ist hier has.
items.note: Single word 'has' — the third-person form learners forget. German cue, no English jargon.
